desc_aquadon: A small amphibious Zoid. Weak in direct combat, but easy to train and surprisingly quick.
desc_barigator: Republic amphibious crocodile-type. Replaced the Aquadon as the primary aquatic assault unit. Excels in ambushes — hiding submerged before striking with powerful bite fangs.
desc_command_wolf: A versatile Republic wolf-type Zoid. Well-balanced stats make it reliable in any situation.
desc_crablaster: A tiny crab-type Attack Zoid from the Empire's earliest deployments. Expendable and fragile, but easy to mass-produce for patrol and harassment.
desc_dcsj: The ultimate evolution of the Shield Liger line. Unmatched power and defense, but extremely difficult to master.
desc_dragon_horse: A rare non-combat horse-type Zoid originally used for transport and cavalry. Extremely fast to bond with, but nearly defenseless in battle.
desc_elephantus: Strong and tough, but slow and hard to pilot. Only for the bravest.
desc_garius: Simple and easy to control. Few offensive weapons, but if you take good care of him he will be unstoppable.
desc_geruder: A triceratops-type built to improve on the Malder's survivability. Heavy frontal armor and electromagnetic cannons make it a fearsome charging unit — a precursor to the mighty Red Horn.
desc_giraffsworder: A swift Zoid with blade-like extensions. Devastating attacks, but its thin armor makes it fragile.
desc_glidoler: Fast and strong, but fragile in combat. For experienced pilots.
desc_gorgodos: A sturdy Republic Zoid with thick armor. Slow to train, but it can endure what others cannot.
desc_gunbeetle: A sturdy insect-type Zoid with solid defenses. Not the strongest, but reliable and easy to deploy.
desc_hammerrock: A heavy Empire Zoid that strikes with devastating force. Slow but incredibly tough.
desc_helcat: A stealthy Empire Zoid built for ambushes. Fast and agile with sharp claws, but not very durable.
desc_helldiver: A hammerhead shark-type Attack Zoid equipped with sonar and mini-torpedoes. Small but deadly underwater — a glass cannon that hits hard and breaks easily.
desc_hidocker: A large transport Zoid with thick armor. Very low attack power, but it can take a beating and provides good experience.
desc_hyperscissors: An amphibious crayfish-type designed for swamp operations. Its steel-cutting shears provide defense where the Aquadon provides offense — a sturdy complement in Republic aquatic squads.
desc_red_horn: The Empire's ultimate tank. Devastating firepower and nearly impenetrable armor, but extremely hard to master.
desc_redler: A fast aerial Zoid of the Empire. Deadly attack power, but its light frame makes it vulnerable.
desc_sea_batoras: A horseshoe crab-type Attack Zoid with a low-profile body that evades radar. Remarkably fast in water and tougher than it looks — a stealthy scout for Republic carrier groups.
desc_sea_panther: An amphibious Empire Zoid designed for coastal operations. Tough and resilient, though not particularly fast.
desc_sinker: A sleek aquatic Zoid with high firepower. Glass cannon — hits hard but breaks easily.
desc_spiker: A nimble Republic Zoid that's easy to train and quick to bond with. Perfect for building a strong army fast.
desc_stealth_viper: A snake-type Republic Zoid that excels at quick strikes. Fast and deadly, with moderate durability.
